IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our equipment in your area. This has identified 15 high voltage poles that need replacing and upgrading. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. Our contractors Avidety may carry out preparation works prior to the power cut. During this time, you were connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place in and around North Elham in Folkstone and Hythe. A road closure will be in place to allow the work to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the Messaging Team here at UK Power Networks. 

You may notice that we have less of our teams, vehicles and partners in the Colworth Road area after today. I wanted to offer you my personal reassurance that even though we have less physical presence on site, our partners, Haste, will still be working to visit each property to repair or replace damaged equipment.

Should you need to speak to Haste, they can be contacted on 01730 897100. As this will be the last message from my team, I wanted to extend a final and sincere apology for the inconvenience caused by what has been a very uncommon and complex fault with our network. Thank you.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a final update to advise that our Customer Champions, the food van and Customer Support vehicle have all now left site.

They will be returning to the area from 9am tomorrow morning and we will let you know. If you have any further questions or concerns this evening though, please do call us on 105 and we will do our best to provide support. Sent at 20:23 by Will.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Good evening. Just a quick update to advise that our Customer Champions will be leaving the area at 20:00 and then return tomorrow morning from approx 09:00.

They are still based in the Claremont Road at the moment though so please do visit them if you need any information or support at all. If you are unable to make it to them though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105 or simply 105 and we will be able to get a message to them.

What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.
